evals.prompt: I have a UserService class in a NestJS app with methods: register(email, password, name), login(email, password), and resetPassword(email). Write comprehensive tests for this service using Jest. The service uses a UserRepository for database operations and BcryptService for password hashing.
evals.expected_output: Jest test file with AAA pattern, test factories, mock setup for repo and bcrypt, tests covering happy path, validation errors, duplicate email, wrong password, nonexistent user, and edge cases

assertions.name: uses_arrange_act_assert
assertions.description: Tests follow AAA pattern with clear sections

assertions.name: mocks_at_boundary
assertions.description: Mocks UserRepository and BcryptService, not internal methods

assertions.name: covers_happy_path
assertions.description: At least one test per method for successful operation

assertions.name: covers_error_cases
assertions.description: Tests for duplicate email, wrong password, user not found

assertions.name: uses_factory_or_builder
assertions.description: Uses a factory function or builder for test data rather than inline objects everywhere

assertions.name: no_implementation_testing
assertions.description: Does not spy on private/internal methods

evals.prompt: Using TDD, implement a ShoppingCart class that supports: addItem(product, quantity), removeItem(productId), getTotal(), and applyDiscount(code). Write the tests first, then the implementation. Use TypeScript with Jest.
evals.expected_output: Tests written BEFORE implementation. Red-Green-Refactor cycle visible. Minimal implementation code. Tests cover add, remove, total calculation, discount codes, edge cases (empty cart, invalid discount).

assertions.name: tests_before_implementation
assertions.description: Test code appears before or in separate file from implementation

assertions.name: red_phase_visible
assertions.description: Tests reference types/interfaces that don't exist yet (showing RED phase)

assertions.name: minimal_implementation
assertions.description: Implementation is minimal - no extra features beyond what tests require

assertions.name: refactor_phase
assertions.description: Evidence of refactoring step (extracted helpers, improved naming)

assertions.name: incremental_tests
assertions.description: Tests are written one behavior at a time, not all at once

assertions.name: edge_cases_tested
assertions.description: Empty cart, negative quantity, invalid discount code tested

evals.prompt: Generate API tests for a REST API with these endpoints: POST /api/v1/products (create), GET /api/v1/products (list with pagination), GET /api/v1/products/:id (get by ID), PUT /api/v1/products/:id (update), DELETE /api/v1/products/:id (delete). The API uses JWT auth with admin and user roles. Only admins can create/update/delete. Use Supertest with NestJS.
evals.expected_output: Complete E2E test file covering all 7 test categories (CRUD, auth, validation, errors, pagination, rate limiting, authorization). Role-based tests for admin vs user. Error response format validation.

assertions.name: covers_all_endpoints
assertions.description: Tests exist for all 5 endpoints (POST, GET list, GET by ID, PUT, DELETE)

assertions.name: tests_auth
assertions.description: 401 tests for unauthenticated requests on protected endpoints

assertions.name: tests_authorization
assertions.description: 403 tests showing regular users cannot create/update/delete

assertions.name: tests_validation
assertions.description: 400 tests for missing/invalid fields on POST and PUT

assertions.name: tests_not_found
assertions.description: 404 test for non-existent product ID

assertions.name: tests_pagination
assertions.description: Tests for page/limit params and response meta

assertions.name: consistent_error_format
assertions.description: Error responses validated for standard format (statusCode, error, message)

evals.expected_output: Simplified version using guard clauses, named constants, extracted helper functions, proper TypeScript types, filter/map instead of manual loop, and clear naming.

assertions.name: reduces_nesting
assertions.description: Maximum nesting depth reduced from 5 to 2 or less

assertions.name: uses_guard_clauses_or_filter
assertions.description: Uses early returns, filter(), or guard clauses instead of nested ifs

assertions.name: extracts_constants
assertions.description: 86400000 replaced with a named constant like MS_PER_DAY

assertions.name: adds_types
assertions.description: Replaces 'any' with proper TypeScript interface/type

assertions.name: extracts_helper
assertions.description: Name formatting logic extracted to a separate function

assertions.name: uses_modern_syntax
assertions.description: Uses const/let, template literals, or array methods instead of manual loop
